title: Thematic Mapping: Georgetown, Guyana as a Top-Class Caribbean City
excerpt: Visualizing urban density, zoning, and infrastructure to understand Georgetown’s potential and challenges as a regional capital.
content:
Georgetown is transforming. With the oil and gas boom, the city is experiencing rapid verticalization and sprawl. Thematic mapping provides the lens through which we can understand this change.

Key Indicators Mapped

  • Commercial Density: The shift of business districts southwards.
  • Traffic Flow: Choke points in the classic grid layout.
  • Green Spaces: The preservation of canals and gardens.

By comparing Georgetown’s spatial metrics with other Caribbean capitals like Kingston and Port of Spain, we can better advocate for zoning policies that preserve its character while enabling growth.
